note: Sprint 5.7: Trinity Useful Compute Worker v0.1 (local-dry-run). Adds scripts/trinity/useful_compute_worker.py with 6 deterministic placeholder task handlers (dft/quantum/structure_relaxation/scoring/simulation/other); schemas/trinity/useful_compute_result.schema.json (strict, safety_status all true); evolves website/trinity-useful-compute.html from static mockup to guided local-worker panel (Load Request, Validate, Show CLI command, Load Result, View Pending Reward) with no network primitives and no sensitive input fields; docs/TRINITY_USEFUL_COMPUTE_WORKER_V01.md. The worker rejects --broadcast, --payout, --send, --wallet, --network and --worker-id-from-wallet; only --mode local-dry-run is accepted. Pending rewards only — no on-chain registration, no automatic payout, no wallet access. Determinism: output byte-identical across runs for same (request_id, worker_id, input_bundle_sha256). Tests: 40 new across tests/trinity/test_{useful_compute_worker,useful_compute_result_schema,useful_compute_web_safety}.py; static safety surface and no-network checks extended to cover the worker; trinity suite 238/238 PASS with materials-engine-private present, 168 PASS + 70 SKIP without. No consensus, RPC, node, miner, wallet or wire-format change.
